avoid: Do not replace the W @ X full GEMM with a custom CUDA GEMM; direct cuBLAS SGEMM should remain responsible for the main term.
Do not add explicit B.T materialization, input validation, contiguity copies, dtype checks, or explicit d range checks in the optimized trusted-input path.
Do not pursue block-size or float4-store tuning unless there is actually a handwritten rank-16 kernel; this candidate should remain pure cuBLAS.
Do not hardcode hidden dimensions beyond using the runtime d and fixed rank 16.
Do not use result caching as an optimization unless the benchmark explicitly validates repeated identical inputs as the intended workload.
Do not promote based only on aggregate speedup when one supported shape regresses noticeably.
evidence: Correctness passed for all tested d values: 3584, 4096, and 4608, with max_abs_err 0.00146484375 and rel_l2_err 7.91e-07.
The candidate compiles successfully and static review passed; warnings about no dtype/contiguity/TORCH_CHECK validation are acceptable for the trusted-input minimal cuBLAS target.
Aggregate speedup is essentially neutral at 1.0045x.
Shape results are mixed: 3584 improves by 1.044x, 4608 improves by 1.037x, but 4096 regresses to 0.951x.
Latency is dominated by the large d x d main GEMM W @ X, so replacing it with anything other than direct cuBLAS SGEMM is unlikely to help.
The intended pure three-SGEMM structure is the right direction: no explicit B.T materialization, no ATen mm/add path, and beta=1 accumulation for the low-rank term.

recommended_actions: try float4 stores when d is divisible by 4
fall back to ATen if numerical error appears
Do not promote unconditionally because the 4096 case has a meaningful regression despite small wins at 3584 and 4608.
Use this candidate as a validated pure-cuBLAS reference if the implementation exactly follows the /workspace/lora/3_sgemm.cu mapping.

Verify the exact SGEMM contract: SGEMM1 computes Y = W @ X using cuBLAS row-major mapping; SGEMM2 computes U with shape {d,16} using opA=N/opB=T, m=d, n=16, k=d, ldc=d; SGEMM3 accumulates A @ U.T or equivalent low-rank update into Y with m=d, n=d, k=16 and beta=1.

Confirm the code uses CUDAGuard and at::cuda::getCurrentCUDABlasHandle, and passes tensor.data_ptr<float>() directly into cublasSgemm without validation checks, input copies, explicit d range checks, or explicit B.T materialization.
Run more stable timing with more iterations and per-kernel profiling to determine whether the 4096 regression is benchmark noise, cuBLAS algorithm selection variance, or low-rank SGEMM overhead.
If the 4096 regression persists, use shape-aware dispatch: this candidate for 3584 and 4608, and the existing torch/ATen path or a separately validated path for 4096.
If any implementation deviation exists, first simplify back to the minimal trusted-input three-SGEMM cuBLAS version before attempting custom kernels or additional tuning.
